steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a verb-form question. The blank sits between "yellow" and "considered complementary to blue" — the main clause has no finite verb yet, so the blank must supply one.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'is': The finite present-tense verb "is" pairs with the past participle "considered" to form the passive "is considered," giving the clause a proper finite main verb. ✓
  • Choice B — 'having been': "Having been" is a nonfinite perfect participle; it cannot serve as the finite main verb of a clause. ✗
  • Choice C — 'to be': "To be" is a nonfinite to-infinitive; it cannot serve as the finite main verb of a clause. ✗
  • Choice D — 'being': "Being" is a nonfinite present participle; it cannot serve as the finite main verb of a clause. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
